problem_statement: A passenger who has cancelled a booking with an unresolved pending refund can immediately create another booking on the same flight. The duplicate/refund guard is checking the wrong booking status, so cancelled bookings are being skipped instead of still blocking re-booking while refund_status is pending.
goal: Ensure the booking creation guard rejects a new booking for the same passenger and flight whenever there is any unresolved refund tied to that passenger/flight pair, returning HTTP 409 until the refund is resolved.
acceptance_criteria: Creating a booking for passenger A on flight F must fail with 409 if passenger A has any existing booking for flight F whose refund_status is pending/unresolved, even if that booking is cancelled.
The duplicate/refund conflict check must not ignore cancelled bookings when refund_status is still pending.
Once the refund is resolved, the passenger should be able to book the same flight again according to existing duplicate rules.
constraints: Smallest relevant implementation area is backend/app/services/booking_service.py, specifically _ensure_no_duplicate_or_refund_conflicts.
Behavior should preserve existing duplicate-booking protections except for the incorrect status filter.
Likely needs/should be covered by service-level tests in backend/tests/services/test_booking_service.py.
unknowns: Exact booking/refund status enum names used by the service (e.g., cancelled, rescheduled, pending, resolved).
Whether the bug is in query filtering, in-memory conditionals, or both inside _ensure_no_duplicate_or_refund_conflicts.
Whether current tests already cover the opposite case (rescheduled bookings) and need adjustment or new regression coverage.
repo_summary: Backend-focused monorepo with booking and flight services; issue points directly to booking_service.py and service tests.

targeted_symbols.reason: The reported bug explicitly names this helper as the location where cancelled bookings are skipped incorrectly; this is the likely point where the refund/duplicate filter needs to be corrected.

localization_rationale: The issue description already names the affected file and helper, so localization is strong.
The symptom is a booking creation allowlist/denylist bug tied to refund state, which is typically enforced in the service-layer conflict check rather than the API route or schema.
Service tests are the most relevant secondary location for reproducing and locking in the fix.
